summary: Requirement typing migrates to `package = "<name>"` — the `contract` facet (template path / inline clauses) retires; satisfiers validate against the named package

edit.description: `RequirementContract` (Template path | inline clauses) becomes a by-name package binding: `package = "<name>"` on `[requirement.*]`. Inline clauses under a requirement retire — `10-contracts.md` typing is `kind`/`package`, 'never inline clauses (clauses live only in packages)'; the unknown-key reject catches stragglers.

Conformance validates a requirement's satisfiers against the named package (resolved via PACKAGE-BINDING's order); admissibility's clause becomes 'names a real kind/package' — a non-resolving name is admissibility's finding, whether it is *filled* stays coverage's. `template` vocabulary sweep in docs/messages.
Typed-requirement fixtures migrate from `contract = "contracts/*.toml"` / inline clauses to `package = "<name>"` over package fixtures; admissibility cases (non-resolving name) updated.
Requirement parse fixtures: the `contract`/inline forms are now unknown-key rejects; `package` parses.
Any requirement-side template-path case migrates to the by-name binding.

tests.asserts: a requirement typed `package = "<name>"` validates its satisfiers against that package (findings retagged per satisfier); a non-resolving name fails admissibility; `contract =` and inline clauses are rejected keys.

acceptance: Requirement typing is `kind`/`package` exactly as `10-contracts.md` declares — by-name, exact, never inline; packages compose (a satisfier is checked by its kind's bound package and any requirement-named package); cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: Completes the require-side vocabulary migration: after this no `template`/`contract`-as-bundle usage remains in src (`contract` stays as the clause-set a package carries, per `90-spec-system.md` naming). Serialized — shares compose.rs/roster.rs with the entries above.

summary: A member is one document — import projects a `+++` clause-module header ([clause.*], [satisfies.*], [edge.*], [provenance]) over the body; the meta.toml+body split retires

per.section: Decision: the member is one document in the surface language

edit.description: Project each skill/rule/custom unit as ONE document — `<into>/<kind-dir>/<name>/{SKILL,RULE,<KIND>}.md`, a `+++` TOML header (clause modules) over the content-faithful body — replacing the `meta.toml` + body pair; import stays a migration (mechanical, members arrive unrecognized, provenance stamped); the `lock.toml` roll-up unchanged.

`to_meta_document`/`from_dir` become header emit/parse over the document primitive: `[clause.<field>]` modules for typed fields (unknown frontmatter keys preserved verbatim), `[satisfies.<req>]` modules (each with the optional authored `rationale` the spec example carries), `[provenance]` generated.
Same migration for the rule member document, including the structured edge field as `[edge.<target>]` modules (`45-governance.md`: an edge is a declared field on the surface).
Custom-unit read follows the document form (provenance from the header, body below) — still provenance-only pending COVERAGE-CUSTOM-KIND for authored `satisfies`.
Fingerprints / three-state comparison range over the one-document member instead of the meta.toml pair.
Workspace load reads member documents (extraction ranges over the member document — `15-kinds.md`, the adapter).
Projection re-emits from the member document; determinism/idempotence snapshots re-recorded.
`re-add` lifts an on-disk edit into the member document's header/body.
Import/check end-to-end fixtures and snapshots over the new layout.
The import fixpoint (re-importing the projection yields the surface back) asserted over the one-document layout.

retire: the meta.toml + body two-file member layout
Skill::to_meta_document / the rule meta writer (become document emitters)

tests.asserts: import writes one document per member (header modules + verbatim body); re-import is idempotent (fixpoint snapshot); drift/re-add/apply operate over the single file; companions still copied byte-for-byte; snapshots re-recorded deliberately.

acceptance: `.temper/<kind-dir>/<name>/` holds one authored document per member — exactly the `20-surface.md` topology; no meta.toml is written or read; import→check→drift round-trips green; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: ATOMIC and the chain's largest tick — write and read sides must flip together (import writes what check/drift/re-add consume); stay green, re-record insta deliberately. The inbox's representation hold is lifted: the human authored this model into `20-surface.md` (9aed8cc) after routing the hold, and the resolved sequencing fork directs plan to reconcile against that corpus.

summary: A custom kind is authored under `.temper/kinds/<name>/` and registered in the assembly — the fully-inline `[kind.<name>]` definition retires; its require-side is a bound package

per.section: Decision: a custom kind is an authored `.temper/` artifact, registered in the assembly

edit.description: `[kind.<name>]` for a custom kind becomes a registration — a pointer to the authored definition under `.temper/kinds/<name>/` plus its `package = "<name>"` binding — never the inline `governs`/`[[extraction]]`/`[[clause]]` definition (the `CustomKind` parse moves to the artifact loader); a custom kind carries no clauses itself.

Load the composed definition (governs locus, extraction primitives, relationships) from the authored `.temper/kinds/<name>/KIND.md` — the one-document, same-medium format the resolved `(kind-artifact-format)` fork settled (`20-surface.md`: header carries the definition, body the kind's prose).
Discovery unchanged in principle (kinds come from the assembly's registrations) — re-point at the artifact-loaded definitions.
Custom-kind fixtures move from inline `[kind.<name>]` definitions to `.temper/kinds/<name>/` fixtures + registrations; inline definition keys become rejects.
Custom-kind fixtures follow the registration form.
Relationship fixtures declared in the authored kind definition.

retire: the fully-inline [kind.<name>] custom-kind definition (governs/extraction/clauses in temper.toml)

tests.asserts: a custom kind authored under `.temper/kinds/<name>/` and registered in the assembly extracts, checks against its bound package, and yields its declared relationships; an inline definition is rejected.

acceptance: Custom kinds live as authored `.temper/kinds/<name>/` artifacts registered in the assembly, require-side always a bound package — uniform with built-ins; no inline kind definition parses; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: `(kind-artifact-format)` RESOLVED (7e462e7): a custom kind is authored as `.temper/kinds/<name>/KIND.md` — one document, same surface medium (`20-surface.md`, Decision). Downstream of MEMBER-DOCUMENT-IMPORT (the document medium it is authored in); serialized behind it on the shared compose.rs/import.rs surface.

summary: Embed the built-in std-lib from temper's own `.temper/packages/` PACKAGE.md sources and retire `contracts/*.toml` — the dogfood un-park that validates the reconciled machinery

per.section: Decision: a package is project-authorable, not vendor-privileged — and is itself a kind

gate.reason: the authored sources must exist first: a human un-parks the cb52cc3 self-application surface and authors `.temper/packages/{skill.anthropic,rule}/PACKAGE.md` on temper's own surface — the resolved `(package-surface-sequencing)` order (machinery first, dogfood as validation); also last in the chain (needs PACKAGE-DOCUMENT…KIND-AUTHORED-ARTIFACT shipped)

new.description: The authored built-in package sources on temper's own surface — human/dogfood territory; the embed target replacing `contracts/*.toml`.
Embed the authored package directories into the binary — a sanctioned-crate addition (include_dir / build.rs codegen), folded in per the resolved fork.

edit.description: Built-in names resolve from the embedded PACKAGE.md sources instead of `include_str!("../contracts/*.toml")` (main.rs:59/65).
The plugin ships the embedded PACKAGE.md std-lib verbatim instead of the TOML files.
Retire the TOML-file contract loader once nothing resolves through it.

tests.asserts: built-ins resolve from the embedded authored sources; the bundled plugin carries them; nothing references contracts/*.toml.

acceptance: One artifact, two provenance roles: the same PACKAGE.md is authored in temper's repo and shipped embedded; `contracts/` is gone; `temper check` runs green on temper's own `.temper/`; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: The dogfood finish of the chain — un-parking `.temper/` is the *validation* step, never tangled into the machinery (resolved fork). Blocked on human authoring; the embedded `contracts/*.toml` floor persists until then by design.

summary: Custom-kind units join coverage — read authored `satisfies` from the member document instead of hardcoding it empty

gate.reason: follow-on; no story yet demands custom-kind coverage participation, and it is downstream of MEMBER-DOCUMENT-IMPORT (the header its `satisfies` would be authored in) and KIND-AUTHORED-ARTIFACT — deferred on priority

edit.description: Populate `Features.satisfies` for a custom-kind `Unit` from the member document's `[satisfies.*]` modules rather than the hardcoded `satisfies: Vec::new()` (kind.rs:451-454, inside `Kind::extract`) — coverage is kind-blind by design, so temper's own `spec` corpus can opt into requirements. Likely a deliberate extraction-algebra addition (`specs/15-kinds.md`).
A custom-kind unit declaring `satisfies` covers a requirement, exactly as a skill/rule does.

tests.asserts: a custom-kind unit's authored `satisfies` participates in coverage (kind-blind).

acceptance: A custom-kind unit's authored `satisfies` participates in coverage exactly as a skill/rule does; temper's own `spec` corpus can opt into requirements; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: kind.rs:451-454 hardcodes `satisfies=Vec::new()` in `Kind::extract` (confirmed on disk). Under the surface-language model the read is from the member document's `[satisfies.*]` header modules (no meta.toml projection anymore). Re-scope on revival against the then-current chain state.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than blindly clobbering the harness manifest.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB: root `package.json` already exists as the *private* flume manifest — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Parked until a human sets creds up.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` imported as one AGENT.md member document, checked by an authored agent package

gate.reason: the reframe deprioritizes more built-in kinds; revive only if a story demands the agent kind — and only after the surface-language chain (through MEMBER-DOCUMENT-IMPORT) reshapes how a kind's members and package land

new.description: The Agent IR — the typed model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs`: typed YAML frontmatter on the projection side (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`), unknown keys preserved, content-faithful body, provenance. Identity is the path (file stem). The surface member is one AGENT.md document (clause-module header) like every kind.

The agent kind's authored built-in package (clauses + guidance) in the one-document medium, embedded with the std-lib (the EMBED-BUILTIN-PACKAGES model — NOT a contracts/agent.toml). Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`.
Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an insta import round-trip proving idempotence.

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this entry owns the line).
Extend `Workspace` with `agents` loaded under `<dir>/agents/*` and a `WorkspaceError` arm; update the load tests.
Add `agent_features` mirroring `rule_features`.
Discover `.claude/agents/*.md`, project each to one `<into>/agents/<name>/AGENT.md` member document, add an `[[agent]]` lock row per agent.
Bind the agent package, wire admissibility + conformance streams and `by_kind` for the kind.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the agent package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred by the requirements/satisfies reframe: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction. Rewritten to the surface-language shapes — one AGENT.md member document, an authored PACKAGE.md, no meta.toml, no contracts/agent.toml. On revival its shared-surface blast radius (lib/check/extract/import/main) serializes against any then-open entry.
